title: Questions about podman 1.4.3
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**


/kind feature

**Description**


Currently podman 1.4.0 released via package on atomic ppa.  However 1.4.3 is already in release. 
One of the features is a much better performance in I/O heavy environments.
1.  What does it mean compared to 1.3.2?  I currently run ceph in podman and it is I/O intensive.
2.  Will 1.4.3 package built any time soon?

comments.body:
I'll poke @lsm5 about a fresh release on the PPA.

On a test system with 100% pegged I/O on the partition Podman was using, we saw execution times for simple Podman commands go down from ~90 seconds to ~4 seconds. This is mostly accomplished through streamlining the fastpath - there are still many things which will cause slower performance (for example, launching containers will not be significantly sped up - but commands like `ps` or `inspect` will be).
@lsm5 the build doesn't appear on PPA yes

On Thu, Jun 27, 2019 at 10:14:21AM -0700, alitvak69 wrote:
> @lsm5 the build doesn't appear on PPA yes

It will, once it's done, fighting some other fires too, I'll let you know once
the build is ready


-- 
Lokesh
IRC, GitHub: lsm5
GPG: 0xC7C3A0DD
https://keybase.io/lsm5
Can we build our own debian package? I also need to upgrade podman to version 1.4.2, but only 1.4.0 in ppa.

I just built 1.4.3 for 18.04 (16.04 to follow), should be published in a bit.

@mritd in case you're interested in building your own or if you want to package it for vanilla debian, untar [this tarball](https://launchpad.net/~projectatomic/+archive/ubuntu/ppa/+sourcefiles/podman/1.4.3-1~ubuntu18.04~ppa1/podman_1.4.3-1~ubuntu18.04~ppa1.tar.xz) and look in the `debian/` directory.

@lsm5 Thank you for your help.

I am not familiar with the debian package build, I just used [fpm](https://github.com/jordansissel/fpm) to create the debian package. Does the official have tools such as scripts for quickly creating installation packages for different Linux distributions? (e.g. `make package`)
